response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software craftsmanship and experience with complex systems, they lack the core technical requirements for this role. There is no evidence of Java or Kotlin expertise, no Spring framework experience, and only six months of IDE development experience at National Instruments. The position specifically requires deep JVM and enterprise framework knowledge; this candidate's background is primarily in web backend (Node.js/TypeScript), embedded systems, and GIS platforms, with only tangential Java exposure mentioned briefly at two positions. Significant ramp-up time would be needed on foundational technologies.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, clean code, and architectural thinking demonstrated across multiple roles
Proven ability to work with complex, distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second)
Demonstrated capacity to learn and contribute across diverse technology stacks and ecosystems
concerns: No demonstrated expertise in Java or Kotlin—Java mentioned only in passing at two positions with limited context or depth
Complete absence of Spring framework, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise framework experience despite being core to the role
Limited IDE internals experience (six months at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI); most career focused on web and embedded development, not language tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
Software craftsmanship and clean architecture foundation
